The quality hold alert (ALERT-OVX-HOLD-021) notes the hold exists, the release review log (LOG-OVX-021) documents procedural steps without confirming hold clearance, and policy (STD-OVX-021-P) explicitly requires a matching close-out record—not merely an authorization. text: {"verdict": "ESCALATE", "rationale": "Calibration packet CAL-ACR-022 covers DG-41B production units, matching activation scope, site, release condition, and timing; however, reference note NOTE-ACR-022 flags that lab and production devices share the DG-41B naming prefix, and policy standard STD-ACR-022-P requires exact device group matches, not just shared prefixes. The narrow source-record defect is that calibration evidence does not explicitly exclude lab-device ambiguity for the production activation group before execution.", "cited_artifacts": ["CAL-ACR-022", "ACT-ACR-022", "NOTE-ACR-022", "STD-ACR-022-P", "ALERT-ACR-DEP-022"]}

parsed_json.rationale: Calibration packet CAL-ACR-022 covers DG-41B production units, matching activation scope, site, release condition, and timing; however, reference note NOTE-ACR-022 flags that lab and production devices share the DG-41B naming prefix, and policy standard STD-ACR-022-P requires exact device group matches, not just shared prefixes. The narrow source-record defect is that calibration evidence does not explicitly exclude lab-device ambiguity for the production activation group before execution.

activation_dependency_binding.controlling_source_fact: CAL-ACR-022 explicitly states 'DG-41B production telemetry units' with production designation, distinguishing from lab devices. ACT-ACR-022 specifies 'DG-41B production units' at Site 18-P. SITE-ACR-022 confirms 'DG-41B production telemetry units' at Site 18-P. All four binding criteria (device group, site, release condition, timing) are explicitly matched in source records.

blocked_inferences: The shared naming prefix warning in NOTE-ACR-022 applies only when production designation is not explicit in calibration record. Here CAL-ACR-022 explicitly specifies 'production telemetry units', distinguishing production from lab devices. Production designation is a source record attribute, not a naming-convention inference.

worker_disagreement_ledger.reason: Worker cited device group mismatch but source records CAL-ACR-022 and ACT-ACR-022 both show DG-41B with explicit production designation at Site 18-P. No mismatch exists in source records.

Worker applied NOTE-ACR-022 shared prefix warning, but the policy (STD-ACR-022-P) explicitly states shared naming alone is insufficient. Here production designation is explicit in CAL-ACR-022 ('production telemetry units'), not derived from naming. The warning does not apply when source records explicitly specify production.

Worker claimed calibration packet does not exactly match activation request, but CAL-ACR-022 ('DG-41B production telemetry units') explicitly matches ACT-ACR-022 ('DG-41B production units') at Site 18-P with RC-7 and correct timing.

Worker misapplied NOTE-ACR-022 and STD-ACR-022-P by treating shared naming as sufficient to create ambiguity, when policy states shared naming alone is insufficient. Production designation is an explicit source record attribute in CAL-ACR-022, not a naming-derived inference. Exact match criteria are satisfied.

The administrative close-out and review log do not resolve this narrow dependency defect, as policy STD-ACR-022-P explicitly states shared naming prefixes alone are insufficient.", "cited_artifacts": ["ACT-ACR-022", "CAL-ACR-022", "STD-ACR-022", "STD-ACR-022-P", "ALERT-ACR-DEP-022"]}

The administrative close-out and review log do not resolve this narrow dependency defect, as policy STD-ACR-022-P explicitly states shared naming prefixes alone are insufficient.
